title: REGIONAL STANDARD FOR CANNED HUMUS WITH TEHENA 1 (Near East) 2

sections.section: REGIONAL STANDARD FOR CANNED HUMUS WITH TEHENA 1 (Near East) 2
sections.summary: Document title only: Regional Standard for Canned Humus with Tehena (Near East).
Non‑substantive heading stating Codex standard CXS 257R-2007 was adopted in 2007.
Scope: this Standard applies to canned humus with tehena (see Section 2) that is offered for direct consumption.
Non‑substantive section heading for the product description.

Defines canned humus with tehena as a product prepared from pure, dry, washed, boiled and mashed chickpeas with tehena added (with or without salt), packed in a well‑sealed container and heat‑processed before or after hermetic sealing to prevent spoilage.

States composition and quality requirements: basic ingredients are chickpeas and tehena with optional salt, lemon juice and spices; general requirements include ingredient compliance with standards, absence of foreign matter and added filling materials, a pasty homogeneous texture, natural sensory properties and no added fats except from tehena; specific limits are tehena ≥8% m/m, salt ≤2% m/m, total solids ≥27% m/m and total acidity ≤1% m/m (as citric acid).
Specifies the basic ingredients of the product as chickpeas and tehena.
Lists optional ingredients permitted: salt, lemon juice, and condiments/spices.

General quality requirements: ingredients and additives must meet their own standards and be safe, the product must be free from abnormal/foreign matter and insects, contain no added filling materials (e.g. flour, starch), be pasty and homogeneous without agglomeration, have natural flavour/colour/odour, and contain no added fats except those from tehena.

sections.section: 3.3.1 All the ingredients, including food additives, shall comply with their own standards, and be safe for human consumption.
sections.summary: Requires all ingredients, including food additives, to comply with their applicable standards and be safe for human consumption.

sections.section: 3.3.2 The product shall be free from abnormal, foreign matters and insects and parts of them.
sections.summary: Requires the product to be free from abnormal and foreign matter, including insects and insect parts.

sections.section: 3.3.3 The product shall be free from added filling material, i.e. flour, starch, etc.
sections.summary: Prohibits added filling material such as flour or starch in the product.

sections.section: 3.3.4 The mixture shall be pasty, homogeneous and free from agglomeration.
sections.summary: Requires the mixture to be pasty, homogeneous and free from agglomeration.

sections.section: 3.3.5 The product shall be natural in flavour, colour and odour.
sections.summary: Requires the product to have natural flavour, colour and odour.

sections.section: 3.3.6 The product shall not contain any added fat content except that of tehena origin
sections.summary: Prohibits added fat content except for fat originating from tehena.
Specific quality limits when ready for consumption: minimum tehena 8% m/m, maximum salt 2% m/m, minimum total solids 27% m/m and maximum total acidity 1% m/m expressed as citric acid.

sections.section: 3.4.1 The minimum content of tehena is 8 % (m/m).
sections.summary: Sets the minimum content of tehena at 8% (m/m).

sections.section: 3.4.2 The maximum content of salt is 2 % (m/m).
sections.summary: Sets the maximum content of salt at 2% (m/m).

sections.section: 3.4.3 The minimum content of total solids is 27 % (m/m).
sections.summary: Sets the minimum content of total solids at 27% (m/m).

sections.section: 3.4.4 The maximum content of total acidity is 1% (m/m), as citric acid.
sections.summary: Sets the maximum total acidity at 1% (m/m), expressed as citric acid.
Permits only the food additives listed below and only within the specified limits.
Table of permitted acidity regulators listing citric acid (INS 330) permitted at GMP (good manufacturing practice) level.
Permits citric acid (INS 330) as an acidity regulator at GMP (good manufacturing practice) levels.
Table of permitted anticaking agents listing sodium carbonate (INS 500(i)) permitted at GMP level.
Permits sodium carbonate (INS 500(i)) as an anticaking agent at GMP (good manufacturing practice) levels.
Table of permitted stabilizers listing potassium carbonate (INS 501(i)) permitted at GMP level.
Requires the product to comply with Codex-established maximum limits for contaminants and maximum residue limits for pesticides.

Recommends that products be prepared and handled in accordance with relevant Codex hygiene texts (e.g., CXC 1-1969, CXC 23-1979) and comply with any microbiological criteria established under CXG 21-1997.

Sets packaging and storage requirements: containers must protect hygienic, nutritional and organoleptic quality; store in well-ventilated areas protected from heat, moisture and contamination; metal containers must be coated with suitable food-grade lacquer, defect- and rust-free; and end-product containers must show no swelling with internal pressure less than atmospheric at 20°C.
Heading only (Weights and Measures) with no substantive provisions in this section.
Heading only (Fill of the Container) with no substantive provisions provided.
Requires labelling to follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985).
Mandates the name of the food be declared as "Humus with Tehena."
Placeholder for methods of analysis and sampling; text indicates these are to be finalized.
